title: The Root Loom Chamber

features.name: the-loom-in-darkness
features.desc-on-touch: the loom weaves in such deep darkness that the weaver cannot see the pattern; instead, the darkness learns the loom's language and weaves back

features.name: root-thread-separation
features.desc-on-touch: where individual root-threads learned to cross and recross; the pattern they make underground mirrors languages they've never spoken

features.name: the-pattern-that-fractures
features.desc-on-touch: where tangle begins; the loom tries to hold a pattern and the roots break it intentionally; the breaking is the language

features.name: the-weaver-in-the-roots
features.desc-on-touch: something weaves down here that is neither root nor loom; it learns both their languages and invents a third one in the dark

features.name: the-darkness-that-weaves-back
features.desc-on-touch: the darkness here learned from the loom; it weaves with nothing but absence; the pattern is most visible when you look away

inscription: the loom weaves in darkness; the darkness learns the loom's language; neither knows which is teaching

file.name: Root_Weaving_Undocumented_Patterns_Deep_Record.txt
file.note: fragmentary notes on patterns found underground where loom and root have been weaving together for decades; pattern nomenclature unknown; status: germinating

exits.label: back to the tangle-roots where the fracture was first observed
deeper into the understory where the loom becomes root becomes darkness becomes loom
up toward a forest that remembers the pattern before it broke
a route that was not here yesterday

reverie.title: REVERIE — in the loom chamber underground
narr: you sit beside her in the darkness where the loom works
neither of you can see the pattern but you feel it
she reaches into the threads and pulls out a root
the root has learned words in the space underground
you listen to the loom speak in a language dark as soil
